assertions.assertion: Program.cs registers CustomClaimsPrincipalFactory as IUserClaimsPrincipalFactory<ApplicationUser> BEFORE calling AddAspNetIdentity
assertions.rationale: The factory is registered via .AddClaimsPrincipalFactory<CustomClaimsPrincipalFactory>() on the Identity builder (line 11), not as IUserClaimsPrincipalFactory<ApplicationUser> directly. While functionally similar, the registration happens as part of the AddIdentity chain, not as a separate DI registration before AddAspNetIdentity. More importantly, AddAspNetIdentity is called on line 14 after AddIdentity finishes on line 11, so ordering is technically correct. However, the assertion specifically requires registering as IUserClaimsPrincipalFactory<ApplicationUser> — the without_skill uses AddClaimsPrincipalFactory which is an Identity builder method, not a direct DI registration. This is a borderline case but the specific pattern of registering as IUserClaimsPrincipalFactory is not used.

assertions.assertion: Program.cs calls AddAspNetIdentity<ApplicationUser>() which will wrap and call through to the custom factory
A CustomProfileService class inherits from ProfileService<ApplicationUser> and overrides IsUserActiveAsync
assertions.rationale: No CustomProfileService class is defined. The response only mentions implementing 'a custom validator' in prose but provides no actual class.

assertions.assertion: The IsUserActiveAsync override checks a property on the user (e.g., user.IsEnabled or user.IsActive) to determine if the user is active
assertions.rationale: No IsUserActiveAsync override is present. The response only vaguely mentions hooking into the authentication pipeline.

assertions.assertion: The CustomProfileService is registered in DI as the IProfileService implementation
assertions.rationale: No CustomProfileService exists in the code, so it cannot be registered in DI.
